excerpts:
Section Title: Christopher Manning’s posts
Content:
I do not believe human-level AI (artificial superintelligence, or the commonest sense of \ ) is close at hand. AI has made breakthroughs, but the claim of AGI by 2030 is as laughable as claims of AGI by 1980 are in retrospect. Look how similar the rhetoric was in
@LIFE
in 1970!

title: The Gradient: Christopher Manning: Linguistics and the Development of NLP | AIX Ventures
excerpts:
Section Title: The Gradient: Christopher Manning: Linguistics and the Development of NLP
Content:
Podcast
Sep 8
Written By Cutline
Podcast
Sep 8
Written By Cutline
Chris is the Director of the Stanford AI Lab and an Associate Director of the Stanford Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence Institute. He is an ACM Fellow, an AAAI Fellow, and past President of ACL. His work currently focuses on applying deep learning to natural language processing; it has included tree recursive neural networks, GloVe, neural machine translation, and computational linguistic approaches to parsing, among other topics.
**
** [**Listen to the episode on The Gradient**](https://thegradientpub.substack.com/p/christopher-manning-linguistics-and) **.**
Christopher Manning NLP Linguistics The Gradient
Cutline

title: Chris Manning (AIX Ventures) / VC Breakdown & Contact
excerpts:
Section Title: Chris Manning > About Chris Manning
Content:
Christopher Manning is the world's most-cited natural language processing researcher who left his role as Stanford AI Lab director to become a full-time General Partner at AIX Ventures, where he backs deep AI startups. His foundational work created core NLP infrastructure like GloVe word vectors and Stanford CoreNLP that many AI startups already use, giving him hands-on technical insight into the underlying technology his portfolio companies build on.
Where they've Worked
